title: Meet the Navy Scientist With UFO Patents (Ft. Salvatore Pais)
summary: This interview features Navy scientist Salvatore Pais discussing his exotic propulsion patents, including craft using inertial mass reduction devices and high-frequency gravitational wave generators. Pais explains the theoretical physics behind his work, particularly the 'Pais Effect' involving controlled motion of charged matter through accelerated vibration or spin, and discusses connections to room temperature superconductivity and quantum vacuum engineering.

acronyms.definition: UFO study group referenced in context of 2017 revelations

acronyms.expansion: United States Patent and Trademark Office
acronyms.definition: Federal agency responsible for granting patents

events.description: Revelations around the existence of UFO study groups like OSAP, and Salvatore Pais filed his exotic propulsion patents
Salvatore Pais presented slides at AIAA SciTec conference

key_claims.claim: The Pais Effect involves controlled motion of electrically charged matter based on accelerated vibration and/or accelerated spin, giving rise to extremely high energy densities
Electric fields on the order of 10^18 volts per meter and magnetic fields of 10^9 Tesla are theoretically achievable through the Pais Effect
Three of five patent applications became patents, with the most exotic concepts being granted while more mundane ones were rejected
Bob Lazar described the sports model craft as if it were grown out of the vacuum itself, which aligns with the super force equation
Victor Lakhno of the Russian Academy of Sciences published a paper suggesting Pais's room temperature superconductor mechanism could work
Breaking the Schwinger limit allows creation of black holes which may connect via wormholes to white holes elsewhere
There is both man-made exotic technology and ET technology, and they can be distinguished from each other
The physics behind the patents was available in the 1890s and Pais does not consider himself an original discoverer
Chinese research teams are citing Pais's plasma compression fusion device patent in cybernetics journals as of this year
The Navy experiment achieved only 10^-8 coulombs of charge when at least 1 coulomb is needed to demonstrate the Pais Effect
Townsend Brown's Winter Haven proposal referenced Oliver Heaviside's 1890s theory of gravity as adequate
